The 'represent the early Spark-based work that fed into the Splink package' phrasing slightly overstates a developmental link that the cited source does not establish. The medium confidence and caveat (4) appropriately flag this, so it is a minor overclaim, not a fabrication.

The single source URL per item is sometimes a thin/non-primary page for the 'NOT tied' framing (e.g., the BigMatch working paper, the AIHW services page). The actual ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED corroboration (e.g., AIHW considering Splink as DALI replacement) comes from a secondary source not listed as the item's primary URL. This is a sourcing-hygiene gap, not a false claim.
strongest_items: fastLink (LINEAGE, high) — directly confirmed by MoJ's own gov.uk publication, which states Splink 'builds on fastLink's implementation in R of an Expectation-Maximisation algorithm to estimate a Fellegi-Sunter linkage model.' Correctly tagged as algorithmic predecessor, NOT a runtime dependency (Splink is Python/SQL). Primary source verified.
sparklink (LINEAGE, high) — the GitHub repo literally carries the banner 'THIS REPO HAS MOVED to github.com/moj-analytical-services/splink.' Same maintainers, same project, verified directly.

Fellegi-Sunter 1969 (LINEAGE, high) — canonical theoretical foundation; Splink docs dedicate a theory topic guide to it and gov.uk/MoJ materials describe Splink as estimating an FS model via EM. Correctly framed as conceptual ancestry, not software dependency.

AIHW DALI (ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED, high) — confirmed independent SAS-based tool; the 'AIHW considering replacing DALI WITH Splink' claim is corroborated (realworlddatascience.net case study) and actually reinforces that DALI is a competing tool, not a Splink integration. Relationship tag is correct.

FEBRL and recordlinkage (ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED, high) — verified that both Splink and recordlinkage reuse FEBRL demo datasets, and the segment correctly characterizes this as shared sample data and explicitly NOT a code dependency or integration. The relationship tag is correctly NOT tied.

Match*Pro (ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED, high) — SEER source confirms Fellegi-Sunter framework plus raw-PII and PPRL/hashed-token support; correctly tagged as independent alternative sharing only FS heritage.
items_to_remove_or_downgrade: Apache Spark MLlib — downgrade from ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED to a parenthetical note, or cut. It is not a record-linkage product and not a true alternative; the segment's own caveat admits this. Keeping it as a listed 'alternative' is the loosest fit in the segment.

splink_analysis_match / splink_scalaudfs — keep but retain the medium confidence; soften 'fed into the Splink package' to 'same-org Spark-era artefacts of uncertain developmental relationship,' since the source establishes existence/purpose but not the causal contribution to the released package.
verification.notes: Verified via primary sources: MoJ gov.uk Splink publication (fastLink/FS/EM lineage), sparklink GitHub redirect banner, splink_analysis_match repo description, SEER Match*Pro page, realworlddatascience.net (AIHW DALI->Splink consideration), and Splink/recordlinkage FEBRL demo-data reuse. CONFIRMED: every relationship tag is directionally correct. No ALTERNATIVE_NOT_TIED item is implied to use, depend on, or integrate with Splink — the segment is disciplined about this, explicitly stating each is independent and sharing at most common Fellegi-Sunter heritage. The two demo-data overlaps (FEBRL, recordlinkage) are correctly flagged as shared sample data, NOT integration. No fabricated dependents. Overall the segment is honest and well-hedged; it pre-empts its own weak points in the caveats. Only genuine soft spots are the Spark MLlib category fit and the under-documented causal link for the early MoJ repos, both already flagged at medium confidence by the author. Verdict SOLID rather than perfect because of the MLlib loose fit and per-item primary-source thinness on a few ALTERNATIVE items (corroboration exists but is not always the listed URL).
